title: Lucid Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
text: Lucid Group announced Q1 2026 financial results showing production of 5,500 vehicles, representing a 149% increase year-over-year. The company delivered 3,093 vehicles, flat year-over-year due to February's supplier issue affecting Gravity SUV shipments. January and March deliveries both exceeded prior-year comparisons. Revenue reached $282.5 million, up 20% from Q1 2025. The company reported a net loss of $1.03 billion. On April 14, Lucid announced $1.05 billion in total funding, including $550 million in convertible preferred stock from PIF's Ayar Third Investment Company, $300 million from a common stock offering, and $200 million from Uber, bringing Uber's total investment to $500 million. The company increased its delayed draw term loan capacity by $500 million. Interim CEO Marc Winterhoff stated the company expanded strategic partnerships, including with Uber, and continued advancing autonomy roadmap. Silvio Napoli was announced as the next CEO. Lucid's robotaxi partnership with Uber expanded to at least 35,000 vehicles. Management emphasized focus on disciplined execution and driving structural cost improvements while working toward profitability as production scales.
